passage:
A student is writing a report on the environmental impact of fast fashion. While researching, the student finds the following information:

- The fashion industry accounts for approximately 10% of global carbon emissions.
- A single pair of jeans requires roughly 1,800 gallons of water to produce.
- Synthetic fabrics such as polyester shed microplastic fibers when washed, which enter waterways and accumulate in marine organisms.
- The average American buys more than 65 pounds of clothing per year and discards most of it within a year.
- Fast fashion brands release new collections as frequently as every two weeks, encouraging consumers to purchase more and discard clothing faster.
question: Which sentence most effectively synthesizes the notes to support the claim that fast fashion imposes costs across multiple dimensions of the environment?

choices.A: Fast fashion brands release new collections every two weeks, encouraging American consumers to buy more than 65 pounds of clothing per year.
choices.B: From its high carbon emissions and enormous water consumption to the microplastic pollution generated by synthetic fabrics, fast fashion exerts significant environmental costs across air, water, and ocean ecosystems.
choices.C: A single pair of jeans requires roughly 1,800 gallons of water to produce, illustrating the scale of the fast fashion industry's environmental footprint.
choices.D: The fashion industry accounts for approximately 10% of global carbon emissions, making it one of the largest contributors to climate change.

explanation: The claim requires showing costs across multiple environmental dimensions. B synthesizes three separate environmental categories—air (carbon emissions), water (water consumption), and ocean (microplastic pollution)—and explicitly frames them as costs across multiple domains. A covers only consumer behavior data (collections frequency and pounds purchased), which is relevant to pace of consumption but not directly to environmental cost categories. C covers only water consumption, a single dimension. D covers only carbon emissions, also a single dimension.
